title: viola sororia var. affinis
intro: Viola sororia var. affinis, also known as the Early Blue Violet, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the violet family (Violaceae). The leaves and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Viola sororia var. affinis include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
